mcq_details.question: What is the standard anatomical position for describing body structures?
Which plane is defined as an imaginary vertical line through the center of the body?
The skeletal system's function of providing structural support and protection for vital organs is most effectively achieved by the bones in which plane?
The heart's function is to pump blood throughout the body. Which plane is most relevant to this process?
A histology slide shows a cross-section of the left arm. Which plane would be used to describe its orientation?
What are the bone cells that break down bone tissue called?
What is the sella turcica a feature of?
What is the zygomatic arch formed by?
mcq_details.correct_answer: Zygomatic process of temporal bone and temporal process of zygomatic bone

mcq_details.question: Which bone contains the foramen magnum?
What is the lesser trochanter a feature of?
Which nerve from the brachial plexus innervates the deltoid muscle?
Which meninx is the innermost layer that directly covers the spinal cord?
Which meninx is the outermost and toughest layer?
How many pairs of spinal nerves are there in the human body?
What type of neurons carry sensory information toward the CNS?
What is the function of the cerebellum?
Which brain area is responsible for speech comprehension?
What are arachnoid granulations (villi) responsible for?
What is the largest part of the brain?
What is the arbor vitae?
mcq_details.correct_answer: White matter pattern in the cerebellum

mcq_details.question: What fluid is found in the anterior and posterior chambers of the eye?
What is the choroid?
mcq_details.correct_answer: Vascular layer that nourishes the retina

mcq_details.question: Which cranial nerves carry taste information?
What are the three regions of the ear?
Which cranial nerve carries smell information?
What is the function of cilia in the respiratory tract?
mcq_details.correct_answer: Move mucus and trapped particles toward pharynx

mcq_details.question: What type of cells line the alveoli?
mcq_details.correct_answer: Type I and Type II pneumocytes

mcq_details.question: What are the two functional divisions of the respiratory system?
Which primary bronchus is wider, shorter, and more vertical?
Which bones contain paranasal sinuses?
What is the brachial artery?
mcq_details.correct_answer: Main artery of the upper arm

mcq_details.question: What is the largest artery in the body?
What vessels carry oxygenated blood from the lungs to the heart?
What is the function of the AV node?
mcq_details.correct_answer: Delays impulse to allow atrial contraction before ventricular contraction

mcq_details.question: What type of valves are the aortic and pulmonary valves?
What are the four types of teeth?
What forms the common bile duct?
mcq_details.correct_answer: Union of common hepatic and cystic ducts

mcq_details.question: How many permanent teeth do adults have?
What is the function of the gallbladder?
What connects the esophagus to the stomach?
How does the male urethra differ from the female?
mcq_details.correct_answer: Male urethra is longer and passes through prostate

mcq_details.question: What is the functional unit of the kidney?
Why is transitional epithelium important in the urinary system?
mcq_details.correct_answer: It can stretch as the bladder fills

mcq_details.question: What are juxtamedullary nephrons?
mcq_details.correct_answer: Nephrons with long loops of Henle extending deep into medulla

mcq_details.question: What is the hilum of the kidney?
mcq_details.correct_answer: Indented region where vessels and ureter enter/exit

mcq_details.question: What is the longest muscle in the body?
What is the main muscle of mastication (chewing)?
What is a muscle antagonist?
mcq_details.correct_answer: A muscle that opposes the prime mover

mcq_details.question: What are the three hamstring muscles?
What is the serratus anterior?
mcq_details.correct_answer: Muscle that protracts and rotates the scapula upward
